fields.description:
## Summary
rebar help overview omits nine routable subcommands — including reopen (step 5 of the documented agent loop) and fsck (referenced by CLAUDE.md).

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD)
1. rebar help -> overview lists 32 subcommands.
2. Missing but dispatched and individually documented: reopen, delete, compact, compact-all, fsck, fsck-recover, revert, resolve, format (arms at rebar:248-581; per-subcommand help at rebar:96-134).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: the discovery surface for agents lists every public subcommand.
- Actual: nine omissions; agents relying on help never discover reopen/fsck.

## Source
src/rebar/_engine/rebar:34-73 (_print_overview).

## Fix direction
Add the nine lines with one-line descriptions consistent with their per-subcommand help; add a guard test that extracts every case arm from the dispatcher and asserts each appears in the overview (with a deliberate allowlist for internal-only arms, if any — see implementation-notes comment for the validated extraction approach and the arm inventory it found).

## Suggested RED test
The case-arm-vs-overview guard test is the RED test: it fails today listing the nine, passes after.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All public dispatcher arms appear in rebar help (RED guard test first)
- [ ] Guard test prevents future drift; internal arms (if any) explicitly allowlisted with a comment
- [ ] Descriptions consistent with per-subcommand help text
